programme.title: Live: SportsCenter With Scott Van Pelt
programme.desc: SVP's up at midnight and bringing his unique perspective on games people play to "SportsCenter" viewers. The hourlong, late-night show is driven by the host's self-deprecating wit and passion for sports, with featured elements, including Van Pelt's nightly "1 Big Thing" commentary and a weekly look back at the moments that drove bettors wild in "Bad Beats." programme.title: 2026 Stanley Cup Final
programme.desc: The Vegas Golden Knights and Carolina Hurricanes meet in Game 6 of the Stanley Cup Final. The visiting Hurricanes protect a 3-2 series edge behind goalie Brandon Bussi's 23 saves from Game 5, while Vegas counters with sharp offense on home ice.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. The flagship series airs an evening edition and a brand new midnight show each weekday before the stage is cleared for a live "SportsCenter" morning block that emphasizes fast-paced, highlights-dri

programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: The Pat McAfee Show
programme.desc: Former NFL punter Pat McAfee brings his humor and candor to the microphone to highlight the day's top stories.

programme.title: Live: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: From Charles Schwab Field Omaha in Omaha, Neb.

programme.title: Live: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The Cougars face the Wildcats in a heavyweight Big 12 Men's Basketball Championship final at T-Mobile Center. Both teams are currently ranked high in the Big 12 Conference standings. The winner secures the Big 12 automatic bid in the NCAA Tournament.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 13 Volunteers (7-2) and the No. 14 Fighting Illini (6-2) collide in a Music City Madness matchup at Bridgestone Arena. Tennessee earned a win over Illinois in each of the past two seasons, including a 66-64 decision in 2024-25.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The Volunteers hope to rebound by visiting the Orange for an SEC/ACC Challenge match. Tennessee was 2-1 at the Players Era Festival's in Las Vegas, taking its first loss of the season in the third-place match to Kansas, while Syracuse was 0-3.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 11 Cardinals (9-1) pay a visit to the slumping No. 20 Volunteers (7-3). Tennessee enters this game having lost three straight, to Kansas, Syracuse and Illinois. Louisville looks to avenge a 77-55 loss to Tennessee in last season's meeting.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The No. 19 Tennessee Volunteers host the South Carolina State Bulldogs at the Food City Center. Tennessee (9-3) remains a perfect 7-0 at home this season. They enter this contest coming off a dominant 94-52 victory over Gardner-Webb.

programme.title: Major League Rugby
programme.desc: Semifinal.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 21 Tennessee (11-4) visits Florida (10-5) at the Stephen O'Connell Center. Tennessee senior Ja'Kobi Gillespie (18.7 PPG) faces a Gators squad led by Thomas Haugh (17.5 PPG). Florida seeks a home win in this high-stakes SEC rivalry.

programme.title: 2026 Men's College World Series
programme.desc: From Charles Schwab Field Omaha in Omaha, Neb.

programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: Texas A&M visits No. 21 Tennessee for an SEC battle at the Food City Center. High-scoring guard Jacari Lane anchors the Aggie attack, while standout senior Ja'Kobi Gillespie leads a Volunteers squad fresh off a 34-point performance against Texas.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: From Coleman Coliseum in Tuscaloosa, Ala.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: The Tennessee Volunteers host the Ole Miss Rebels at Food City Center in Knoxville. This SEC matchup has both teams fighting to climb the division rankings. Tennessee's Jakobi Gillespie averages over 18 points per game and leads in scoring.

programme.title: College Basketball
programme.desc: No. 25 Tennessee Volunteers visit Kentucky Wildcats at Rupp Arena. Ja'Kobi Gillespie leads the Tennessee offense against Otega Oweh and the Kentucky defense. Kentucky fights to win at home and climb the divisional ladder in Lexington.

programme.desc: Predator Pursuit TV, hosted by Jeff Thomason, is a rare blend of fast action predator hunting and educational material all rolled into one. Jeff will be taking viewers all over the world chasing a variety of dangerous predators. Prepare to be entertained, and informed, on different tactics to help you become a more successful hunter and put more fur in the back of your truck!

programme.title: Wardens
programme.desc: A busy 4th of July boat party patrol as well as the opening day of firearm deer season and a look behind the counter at the DNR's front office staff.

programme.title: Heartland Waterfowl

programme.title: Our Way of Life
programme.desc: With the arrival of shorter days and cooler temperatures, Heather finishes the year by successfully harvesting a deer before retreating back into her fur shop to sew.

programme.title: Relentless Pursuit

programme.title: Pigman: The Series
programme.desc: Brian Pigman Quaca is a true-blue original-an unapologetic, high-energy host with a larger-than-life personality that has redefined outdoor television. programme.title: MeatEater

programme.title: Backwoods Life
